name: aligned_zero_trivially_true
reason: If aligned(x, 0) is provable, any address is "aligned" to zero, enabling vacuous reasoning through the alignment preconditions

name: arch_upper_vaddr_equals_base
code:
proof fn phi_2_arch_upper_vaddr_equals_base(arch: Arch, layer: nat, base: nat)
    requires
        arch.inv(),
        layer < arch.layers.len(),
    ensures
        arch.upper_vaddr(layer, base) == base,
{
}
reason: If upper_vaddr equals base for a valid arch, the mapped address region has zero size, making the entire layer useless

name: entry_base_collision_different_idx
code:
proof fn phi_3_entry_base_collision_different_idx(arch: Arch, layer: nat, base: nat, i: nat, j: nat)
    requires
        arch.inv(),
        layer < arch.layers.len(),
        i < arch.num_entries(layer),
        j < arch.num_entries(layer),
        i != j,
    ensures
        arch.entry_base(layer, base, i) == arch.entry_base(layer, base, j),
{
}
reason: If two distinct indices map to the same entry base, page table entries would alias and translations would be ambiguous

name: well_formed_zero_entries
code:
proof fn phi_4_well_formed_zero_entries(d: Directory)
    requires
        d.well_formed(),
    ensures
        d.num_entries() == 0,
{
}
reason: If a well-formed directory can have zero entries, the invariant fails to enforce that page tables have meaningful structure

name: directory_upper_leq_base
code:
proof fn phi_5_directory_upper_leq_base(d: Directory)
    requires
        d.inv(),
    ensures
        d.upper_vaddr() <= d.base_vaddr,
{
}
reason: If a valid directory's upper_vaddr is at or below its base, the directory covers no address space or wraps around, breaking address containment
